statute: Nebraska Revised Statutes § 48-2101 et seq. (Contractor Registration); § 54-301 (Mechanics Lien)
license_notes: Nebraska requires registration for contractors earning over $5,000 annually. Electrical contractors require state license from Nebraska State Electrical Division. Plumbing and HVAC may require local licenses.

mandatory_clauses.label: Nebraska contractor registration
Mechanics lien notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE OF LIEN RIGHTS

Under state law, contractors, subcontractors, and material suppliers who provide labor or materials for your property and are not paid may have the right to file a mechanic's lien against your property. Even if you pay your general contractor in full, you may still be subject to liens from unpaid subcontractors and suppliers. Protect yourself by obtaining written lien waivers from all parties before making final payment.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)
Not legal advice disclaimer
mandatory_clauses.text: DISCLAIMER: This contract was generated by ContractorDesk as a starting point template. It does not constitute legal advice. Contractors are encouraged to have a licensed attorney review agreements for their specific situation and applicable state requirements.

penalties_for_non_compliance: Citation up to $500 for non-registration; 60-day grace to register and waive penalty. Bond requirement: $1,000-$100,000 depending on project size.
notes: Nebraska requires registration for contractors with over $5,000 in annual revenue. New contractors have 60 days to register after first citation (waives penalty). Electrical requires state license. Plumbing/HVAC check local (Omaha, Lincoln have specific requirements). $1,000-$100,000 bond depending on license class.
